originalTitle: Conagra (CAG) Stock May Be Below Fair Value As New Product Rollout Lands
summary: Conagra Brands stock has had a tough run over the past few years, yet the current checks suggest the valuation now leans cheap rather than expensive. After years of weak share price returns, the question is whether today’s lower price simply reflects pressure on the business or offers room for a re‑rating. Over the last 5 years, Conagra Brands shares are down about 49%, which has reset expectations and pushed the stock into potential bargain territory for patient investors. The large rollout...
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Conagra Brands stock has had a tough run over the past few years, yet the current checks suggest the valuation now leans cheap rather than expensive. After years of weak share price returns, the question is whether today's lower price simply reflects pressure on the business or offers room for a re‑rating.

Over the last 5 years, Conagra Brands shares are down about 49%, which has reset expectations and pushed the stock into potential bargain territory for patient investors.

The large rollout of nearly 100 new high protein and convenience focused products can support the brand and revenue story over time. At the same time, the shift from the S&P 500 to the S&P 600 highlights the risk that investors may see the company as a smaller, lower growth profile than before.

On Simply Wall St's checks, Conagra Brands is assessed as undervalued in 5 of 6 valuation tests, which means the broader metrics lean toward the stock being cheap rather than fully priced.

The issue now is whether Conagra Brands' weaker long term share performance is a warning sign that the current low valuation is justified, or a window where the market has become too pessimistic on the stock.

Find out why Conagra Brands' -25.8% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Is Conagra Brands a Bargain on Sales?

P/S is a useful way to look at Conagra Brands because food manufacturers often produce steady revenue even when earnings are temporarily noisy. On this metric, Conagra Brands trades on a P/S of about 0.6x, which is very close to the peer average of 0.6x and below the broader Food industry average of 0.9x. That points to a stock that is not being given a premium tag on its sales base, despite its broad portfolio and recent push into new high protein and convenience products.

The Fair Ratio model, which adjusts the expected P/S for Conagra Brands' margins, size and risk profile, sits slightly higher at around 0.7x. With the current multiple below that mark, the stock screens as undervalued on sales, suggesting investors are applying a cautious filter even as the company rolls out nearly 100 new products and refreshes its brand line up. Despite this product expansion and index shift, the market is not pricing Conagra Brands at a premium to sector sales benchmarks.

On the P/S multiple, Conagra Brands currently looks undervalued relative to what its revenue base and risk profile would typically justify.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

The Conagra Brands Narrative: What Would Justify Today's Price?

Simply Wall St Narratives for Conagra Brands sit between the current valuation puzzle and the assumptions that might justify a different price, by spelling out what paths for Conagra Brands' revenue, margins and earnings would need to hold for the stock to be worth materially more or less than it is today on the market. Rather than relying on a single multiple or model result, each narrative lays out its underlying assumptions so you can compare them with the company's actual figures as they are reported. Narratives are available on Simply Wall St's Community page.

Community views on Conagra Brands sit wide apart, with one camp focusing on execution upside and another worried about longer term demand and balance sheet risk.

"Conagra's focused innovations around "better-for-you" and health-oriented product categories, particularly recent moves in protein-centric and plant-based snacks and frozen meals, position the company to capture outsized market share as health and wellness trends accelerate, driving higher topline growth and premium pricing opportunities…"

Read the full Bull Case to see why Conagra Brands could be undervalued

"The ongoing consumer shift toward fresh and minimally processed foods threatens to erode long-term demand for Conagra's core frozen and packaged offerings, increasing the risk of declining sales and impairing the company's ability to sustain top-line revenue growth…"

Read the full Bear Case to see why Conagra Brands could be overvalued

Do you think there's more to the story for Conagra Brands? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

Conagra Brands screens as undervalued on sales based on current P/S checks, with the Fair Ratio model also pointing to a discount rather than a premium. That gap only matters if Conagra can keep its product refresh and high protein focus translating into a resilient revenue base and stable margins. For readers, the crux is whether today's low multiple reflects temporary caution that could ease, or a more lasting reset if concerns about long term demand for packaged and frozen foods prove correct.

This article by Simply Wall St is general in nature. We provide commentary based on historical data and analyst forecasts only using an unbiased methodology and our articles are not intended to be financial advice. It does not constitute a recommendation to buy or sell any stock, and does not take account of your objectives, or your financial situation. We aim to bring you long-term focused analysis driven by fundamental data. Note that our analysis may not factor in the latest price-sensitive company announcements or qualitative material. Simply Wall St has no position in any stocks mentioned.

Companies discussed in this article include CAG .

chineseTitle: 随着新产品的推出，康尼格拉 (CAG) 股票可能低于公允价值
chineseSummary: 康尼格拉品牌的股票在过去几年中经历了艰难的走势，但目前的检查表明，其估值现在倾向于便宜而不是昂贵。经过多年疲软的股价回报，问题是今天的较低价格是否仅仅反映了业务压力或提供了重新评级的空间。过去 5 年里，康尼格拉品牌 (Conagra Brands) 的股价下跌了约 49%，这重新调整了预期，并将该股推向了耐心投资者的潜在低价区域。此次大规模推出...
chineseContent:
使用 Simply Wall St 免费跟踪您的投资，这是受到全球超过 700 万个人投资者信赖的投资组合指挥中心。

康尼格拉品牌的股票在过去几年中经历了艰难的走势，但目前的检查表明，其估值现在倾向于便宜而不是昂贵。经过多年疲软的股价回报，问题是今天的较低价格是否仅仅反映了业务压力或提供了重新评级的空间。

过去 5 年里，康尼格拉品牌 (Conagra Brands) 的股价下跌了约 49%，这重新调整了预期，并将该股推向了耐心投资者的潜在低价区域。

近 100 种新的高蛋白和便利产品的大规模推出可以随着时间的推移支持品牌和收入故事。与此同时，从标准普尔 500 指数转向标准普尔 600 指数凸显了这样的风险：投资者可能会认为该公司的规模比以前更小、增长速度更低。

根据 Simply Wall St 的检查，康尼格拉品牌在 6 项估值测试中的 5 项被评估为低估，这意味着更广泛的指标倾向于该股票便宜而不是完全定价。

现在的问题是，康尼格拉品牌长期疲弱的股价表现是否是一个警告信号，表明当前的低估值是合理的，还是市场对该股变得过于悲观的一个窗口。

了解康尼格拉品牌去年 -25.8% 的回报率为何落后于同行。

康尼格拉品牌是否便宜？

P/S 是查看康尼格拉品牌的有用方法，因为食品制造商即使在盈利暂时出现波动的情况下也常常产生稳定的收入。根据这一指标，Conagra Brands 的市盈率约为 0.6 倍，非常接近同行平均水平 0.6 倍，低于更广泛的食品行业平均水平 0.9 倍。这表明，尽管该股拥有广泛的产品组合，并且最近推出了新的高蛋白和便利产品，但其销售基础并未被赋予溢价标签。

公平比率模型调整了 Conagra Brands 的利润率、规模和风险状况的预期市盈率，略高一些，约为 0.7 倍。目前市盈率低于该水平，该股在销售上被视为低估，这表明尽管该公司推出了近 100 种新产品并更新了其品牌阵容，但投资者仍在谨慎筛选。尽管进行了产品扩张和指数转移，但市场对康尼格拉品牌的定价并未高于行业销售基准。

从市盈率来看，康尼格拉品牌目前的估值相对其收入基础和风险状况通常被低估。

看看数字对这个价格的影响——在我们的估值细分中找到答案。

康尼格拉品牌叙述：什么可以证明今天的价格合理？

康尼格拉品牌的简单华尔街叙述介于当前的估值难题和可能证明不同价格合理的假设之间，通过阐明康尼格拉品牌的收入、利润和收益需要保持哪些路径，才能使股票的价值大幅高于或低于目前的市场价格。每个叙述都列出了其基本假设，而不是依赖于单个倍数或模型结果，以便您可以将它们与公司报告的实际数据进行比较。叙述可在 Simply Wall St 的社区页面上找到。

社区对康尼格拉品牌的看法分歧很大，一个阵营关注执行力的提升，另一个阵营则担心长期需求和资产负债表风险。

“康尼格拉围绕“更好地为你”和以健康为导向的产品类别进行重点创新，特别是最近在以蛋白质为中心、以植物为基础的零食和冷冻食品方面的举措，使该公司能够在健康和保健趋势加速的情况下占领巨大的市场份额，从而推动更高的营收增长和优质定价机会……”

阅读完整的牛市案例，了解为什么康尼格拉品牌可能被低估

“消费者持续转向新鲜和低加工食品，这可能会削弱对康尼格拉核心冷冻和包装产品的长期需求，增加销售额下降的风险，并损害公司维持营收增长的能力……”

阅读完整的熊市案例，了解为什么康尼格拉品牌可能被高估

您认为康尼格拉品牌的故事还有更多内容吗？前往我们的社区看看其他人都在说什么！

根据当前的市销率检查，康尼格拉品牌公司的销售额被列为低估，公平比率模型也表明存在折扣而不是溢价。只有康尼格拉能够保持产品更新和对高蛋白的关注，并将其转化为有弹性的收入基础和稳定的利润率，这种差距才有意义。对于读者来说，关键在于今天的低倍数是否反映了可能缓解的暂时谨慎态度，或者如果对包装和冷冻食品长期需求的担忧被证明是正确的，则反映了更持久的重置。

Simply Wall St 的这篇文章本质上是一般性的。我们仅使用公正的方法根据历史数据和分析师预测提供评论，我们的文章无意提供财务建议。它不构成购买或出售任何股票的建议，并且不考虑您的目标或您的财务状况。我们的目标是为您提供由基本数据驱动的长期重点分析。请注意，我们的分析可能不会考虑最新的价格敏感公司公告或定性材料。 Simply Wall St 没有持有上述任何股票的头寸。

本文讨论的公司包括 CAG。
